answer: A user would pay for both 5-minute and 1-hour cache writes in the same request when both 1-hour and 5-minute cache controls are included in the request, with the 1-hour cache entry appearing before any 5-minute cache entries. The token count is split as follows: cache read tokens are charged for the highest cache hit position (A); 1-hour cache write tokens are charged for the token count between the highest cache hit and the highest 1-hour cache_control block after that (B - A); and 5-minute cache write tokens are charged for the token count between the highest 1-hour cache_control block and the last cache_control block (C - B).

On the third turn, the system checks 20 positions (blocks 35 through 16) for a matching cache entry but finds nothing because the previous cache write was at block 15, which is outside the 20-block lookback window. As a result, there is no cache hit on the third turn if only one explicit breakpoint is used.

Claude Opus 4 (and Claude Opus 4.1 and Claude Opus 3) has the highest cost difference between its base input tokens ($15 / MTok) and cache hits ($1.50 / MTok), with a numerical difference of $13.50 per million tokens.
Enabling web search modifies the system prompt, which invalidates the system and messages cache but does not invalidate the tools cache.

cache_read_input_tokens: 8,000
cache_creation_input_tokens: 1,000
input_tokens: 200
total input token count: 9,200
